condition.selected: The answer could be even or odd

incorrects.description: Student selected 'could be even or odd', missing that 2 guarantees evenness

steps.text: Look at the 2. When you multiply any number by 2, you're skip-counting by 2s. What kind of numbers do you land on when you count by 2s?

incorrects.description: Student selected 'definitely odd', reversing the even-times-anything pattern

steps.text: The 2 tells you you're skip-counting by 2s. When you count by 2s, every number you land on is even. Does that pattern change when you multiply by 7?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights the factor 2 and demonstrates the pattern.

steps.text: Let me show you. When you see a 2 in a multiplication, you know you're skip-counting by 2s. That means every answer you land on is even. 7 times 2 has to be even because the 2 controls the pattern. The answer is definitely even.
